nodes.label: Scroll-Triggered Reveal Animations
nodes.rationale: Animations triggered by Intersection Observer on scroll to create cinematic reveals as users move through vertical chapters — reinforces the storytelling metaphor.

nodes.label: Parallax Background Images
nodes.rationale: Parallax scroll effect on placeholder images across chapters to add depth and immersion to the vertical narrative experience.

nodes.label: Disallow /api/ from Crawlers
nodes.rationale: API endpoints are disallowed from search engine crawling while all frontend pages remain accessible — standard SEO practice for SPAs with server-side API routes.

nodes.label: Cosmological Axis City Design
nodes.rationale: Sultan Hamengkubuwono I designed Yogyakarta's city layout based on a cosmological axis — the imaginary line connecting Mount Merapi, Kraton, and Parangtritis (Southern Sea). This axis is the spiritual and spatial foundation of the city.

nodes.label: Yogyakarta as Independence Revolution Capital
nodes.rationale: When Jakarta fell to the Dutch, Sultan HB IX surrendered his territory's sovereignty to the Republic of Indonesia, making Yogyakarta the heart of the independence revolution and the de facto capital.

nodes.label: Guerrilla Warfare from Goa Selarong
nodes.rationale: Prince Diponegoro waged five years of guerrilla warfare from Goa Selarong cave, draining the Dutch East Indies treasury by 20 million guilders — the longest and most expensive resistance the VOC ever faced in Java.

nodes.label: Sanjaya Dynasty as Zero Point of Written History
nodes.rationale: King Sanjaya's lingam inscription on Mount Wukir (732 CE) is the oldest document of Hindu civilization in the Kedu Plain — the zero point of Yogyakarta's written history.
nodes.norm_label: sanjaya dynasty as zero point of written history

nodes.label: Bilingual Audio Narration (EN/ID)
nodes.rationale: Each historical event has paired English and Indonesian audio narrations, enabling bilingual accessibility for the Sejarah (history) section of JogjaKu.
